title: The Australian Emigrant
content:
آ آ آ آ How dazzling the sunbeams awoke on the spray,
آ آ آ آ When Australia first rose in the distance away,
آ آ آ آ As welcome to us on the deck of the bark,
آ آ آ آ As the dove to the vision of those in the ark!
آ آ آ آ What fairylike fancies appearï؟½d to the view
آ آ آ آ As nearer and nearer the haven we drew!
آ آ آ آ What castles were built and rebuilt in the brain,
آ آ آ آ To totter and crumble to nothing again!

آ آ آ آ We had roamï؟½d oï؟½er the ocean had traversï؟½d a path,
آ آ آ آ Where the tempest surrounded and shriekï؟½d in its wrath:
آ آ آ آ Alike we had rollï؟½d in the hurricaneï؟½s breath,
آ آ آ آ And slumberï؟½d on waters as silent as death:
آ آ آ آ We had watchï؟½d the Day breaking each morn on the main,
آ آ آ آ And had seen it sink down in the billows again;
آ آ آ آ For week after week, till disheartenï؟½d we thought
آ آ آ آ An age would elapse ere we enterï؟½d the port.

آ آ آ آ How often while ploughing the ï؟½watery wasteï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Our thoughts from the Future have turnï؟½d to the Past;
آ آ آ آ How often our bosoms have heavï؟½d with regret;
آ آ آ آ For faces and scenes we could never forget:
آ آ آ آ For weï؟½d seen as the shadows oï؟½er-curtainï؟½d our minds
آ آ آ آ The cliffs of old England receding behind;
آ آ آ آ And had turned in our tears from the view of the shore,
آ آ آ آ The land of our childhood, to see it no more.

آ آ آ آ But when that red morning awoke from its sleep,
آ آ آ آ To show us this land like a cloud on the deep;
آ آ آ آ And when the warm sunbeams imparted their glow,
آ آ آ آ To the heavens above and the ocean below;
آ آ آ آ The hearts had been aching then revellï؟½d with joy,
آ آ آ آ And a pleasure was tasted exempt from alloy;
آ آ آ آ The souls had been heavy grew happy and light
آ آ آ آ And all was forgotten in present delight.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis true of the hopes that were verdant that day
آ آ آ آ There is more than the half of them withered away:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis true that emotions of temperï؟½d regret,
آ آ آ آ Still live for the country weï؟½ll never forget;
آ آ آ آ But yet we are happy, since learning to love
آ آ آ آ The scenes that surround us the skies are above,
آ آ آ آ We find ourselves bound, as it were by a spell,
آ آ آ آ In the clime weï؟½ve adopted contented to dwell.
meaning:
"The Australian Emigrant" is a quintessential example of Henry Kendall's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
